title: rhynchospora pilosa subsp. arenicola
intro: Rhynchospora pilosa subsp. arenicola, also known as coastal hair sedge, is a plant species that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rhynchospora pilosa subsp. arenicola include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are typically used to create teas, infusions, and salves, and it can be found in some traditional herbal remedies, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it medicinally.
